title: Hyundai/Kia Theta II Engine Bearing Failure Detection
description: Hyundai Theta II 2.0T/2.4T engines have a known bearing failure issue (manufacturing debris not cleaned from oil galleries). Covers early detection via knock sensor data, oil pressure monitoring, and recall/warranty check protocol.
common_symptoms: ticking at idle that worsens under load
sudden engine seizure with no warning (worst case)
KSDS (Knock Sensor Detection System) warning light
physical_solution: Check recall/warranty status FIRST (NHTSA recall 17V-226, class action settlement). Monitor knock sensor data via live data. If P1326 or knock activity detected: advise engine replacement under warranty/settlement terms.

uds_services: $22 ReadDataByIdentifier (knock sensor raw data, KSDS status)
Mode $01 PID $0E (timing advance — retard indicates knock)
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — Mode $01 PID $0E (timing advance) shows knock retard as indirect indicator. P1326 readable via Mode $03 or $19. KSDS status and raw knock sensor data require GDS2 or enhanced ELM327 addressing.
preconditions: ALWAYS check NHTSA recall database by VIN first
Oil level and quality check before any diagnosis
Note mileage — warranty extension covers specific mileage ranges

procedure_steps.description: WARRANTY CHECK FIRST: Go to NHTSA.gov recall search or Hyundai/Kia owner portal. Enter VIN. Theta II recall: NHTSA 17V-226 (Sonata/Santa Fe), 17V-224 (Optima/Sportage). Settlement extends warranty to 150k miles or 15 years on affected engines. Never bill customer for Theta II engine repair before confirming warranty status.

Read P1326: Mode $03 or $19 02 FF to ECM. P1326 (KSDS - Knock Sensor Detection System fault) is Hyundai's specific code for metal debris detected in engine via knock sensor pattern analysis. P1326 = engine replacement required. No P1326 = continue monitoring.

Monitor knock retard: Mode $01 PID $0E (timing advance). At cruise (2000-3000 RPM, moderate load): timing should be 15-25° BTDC. Consistent retard below 10° BTDC under moderate load = knock activity = bearing wear.

KNOCK SENSOR RAW DATA: GDS2 shows KSDS analysis results — pattern matching for metal bearing debris signature vs normal combustion knock. Different from standard knock detection. KSDS specifically listens for mechanical bearing noise pattern, not detonation.

OIL INSPECTION: Drain small amount of oil. Check for metallic sheen or particles. Drain plug magnet: metallic particles confirm bearing wear in progress. Fine metallic paste = early bearing failure. Chunky metal = advanced failure — engine may not be salvageable.

ENGINE REPLACEMENT SCOPE (if confirmed): Hyundai/Kia dealer installs remanufactured long block under warranty/settlement. Independent shop option: remanufactured engine from Hyundai ($4,000-6,000 installed). Document everything for warranty claim.

If pre-failure (no P1326, no metal): change oil with OEM Hyundai 5W-20/5W-30 full synthetic immediately. Shorten oil change interval to 3,750 miles. Monitor P1326 and timing retard at each service. Clean oil is the only prevention measure.

oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Affected vehicles: 2011-2019 Hyundai Sonata 2.4L/2.0T, 2013-2019 Santa Fe Sport 2.0T/2.4L, 2011-2019 Kia Optima 2.4L/2.0T, 2011-2019 Kia Sportage 2.4L/2.0T, 2016-2019 Tucson 2.0L/2.4L. Root cause: metal shavings from manufacturing left in engine oil galleries contaminate main/rod bearings. Hyundai implemented KSDS (Knock Sensor Detection System) software update to detect bearing failure before seizure. Settlement: Hyundai agreed to 150k mile/15-year warranty extension for covered vehicles. Class action attorneys: Chimicles Schwartz Kriner & Donaldson-Smith. CRITICAL: this engine is responsible for many sudden engine seizures and fires. Take P1326 seriously — vehicle should not be driven.
